   359→func writeMetrics(rw http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   360→	rw.Header().Add("Content-Type", "application/json")
   361→
   362→	// Extract the "cluster" query parameter without allocating a url.Values map.
   363→	cluster := queryParam(r.URL.RawQuery, "cluster")
   364→

   366→	// temporary buffer via io.ReadAll. The line-protocol decoder supports

content.content: 194:func DecodeLine(dec *lineprotocol.Decoder,

190→//
   191→// When the checkpoint format is "wal" each successfully decoded sample is also
   192→// sent to WALMessages so the WAL staging goroutine can persist it durably
   193→// before the next binary snapshot.
   194→func DecodeLine(dec *lineprotocol.Decoder,
   195→	ms *MemoryStore,
   196→	clusterDefault string,
   197→) error {
   198→	// Reduce allocations in loop:
   199→	t := time.Now()

input.description: Check lineprotocol import in new cc-backend
Check which lineprotocol package cc-backend uses

content.text: Good — both the new cc-backend and this project use `github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-line-protocol/v2/lineprotocol`, so the `DecodeLine` type mismatch in the diagnostics was from before the update. Now I need to fix the `queryParam` issue. Let me check what the old cc-backend version had.

input.description: Check if queryParam existed in old cc-backend

content.text: It wasn't in the old version either. Let me check git log to understand how `queryParam` got introduced.

content.text: The recent commit `b764120` introduced `queryParam` but never defined it. Let me also check if `NewDecoder` (vs `NewDecoderWithBytes`) exists in cc-line-protocol.

Good, `NewDecoder(io.Reader)` exists. The only issue is the missing `queryParam` function. I'll implement it — it's a simple helper to avoid allocating a `url.Values` map, as the comment describes. Alternatively, I can just use the standard `r.URL.Query().Get("cluster")` which is simpler and correct.

Given the comment says "without allocating a url.Values map", let me implement a simple `queryParam` helper:
